response.justification: The candidate lacks the required 5–10 years of strong C++ development experience on Windows platforms, with their C++ background being limited to early career roles (e.g., NI LabWindows/CVI, C components). Their expertise is heavily skewed toward modern web/mobile stacks (TypeScript, Node.js, JavaScript) and other languages (DLang, Java), with no evidence of SQL Server/Oracle experience or capital markets/fixed-income domain knowledge. Domain exposure in investment practices (RIDE Capital) is too brief and not aligned with fixed-income systems.
strengths: Strong multi-language proficiency (TypeScript, JavaScript, DLang, Java)
Experience with CI/CD, TDD, and modern software engineering practices
Proven ability to deliver scalable web/mobile applications
concerns: Insufficient C++ experience on Windows (2 years in early career, not recent)
No demonstrated SQL Server/Oracle expertise (mandatory requirement)
Lacks capital markets, investment banking, or fixed-income domain knowledge
